title: Surveillance of the Patient Who Has Had Therapeutic Hemithyroidectomy for Thyroid Cancer

items.subtopic: Post-hemithyroidectomy surveillance for low-risk papillary thyroid cancer
items.question: A 35-year-old woman with a 1.2-cm left-sided thyroid nodule undergoes hemithyroidectomy and is found to have a 4-mm right lobe spongiform nodule, no abnormal lymph nodes, and a thyroglobulin level of 75 ng/mL (SI: 75 μg/L). What is the best next step?
options: C. Initiation of levothyroxine, 50 mcg daily
D. Continued observation and repeated ultrasonography; measurement of thyroglobulin, thyroglobulin antibodies, and thyroid function in 6 months
E. Completion thyroidectomy followed by radioactive iodine
A. Chest CT to evaluate for metastatic disease
items.explanation: Given the patient's low-risk sonographic nodule size (subcentimeter), her thyroglobulin level, and the absence of abnormal lymph nodes or other worrisome features, continued observation with serial ultrasonography is recommended. Thyroid hormone therapy should be initiated if TSH levels are not at goal (<0.5-2.0 mIU/L). If thyroglobulin remains high despite levothyroxine, additional imaging would be warranted.
items.reference: MAIN CONCLUSIONS: The optimal frequency of follow-up is unknown. Imaging surveillance after hemithyroidectomy is primarily with neck ultrasonography.
